last_item: Band-1.5: restore-test DEEP — UI opt-in button on the Backups page (calls POST /backups/restore-test?deep=true), gated by a confirm dialog that states what it does + costs before running
last_result: done — added a clearly-labeled 'Deep restore test' button next to 'Test a restore' (web/src/views/Backups.tsx), gated behind exported+tested DeepRestoreTestConfirm whose ConfirmDialog copy states up front EXACTLY what the action does and its costs before it runs (actually restores latest backup into a throwaway copy + boots + counts rows; runs LONGER; needs free disk ≈ DB size; live DB never touched; scratch copy deleted; refuses rather than fill disk) — the 'say what will happen before it happens' invariant. Default 'Test a restore' stays the cheap read-only verify. API client runRestoreTest({deep}) appends ?deep=true (web/src/api/client.ts); existing verified_rows>0 'rows restored and verified' result branch already renders the outcome. New vitest/RTL DeepRestoreTestConfirm suite: closed→renders nothing (Modal returns null), copy asserts each material claim (does-what + both costs + safety), confirm/cancel callbacks fire, busy disables. Full web gate green (typecheck/build/37 tests) + full Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test/build, sandbox-disabled per snap). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking issues (it raised 2 concerns, then dismissed both after inspection). dist rebuilt+committed.
notes: Band 1.5 (data durability) NEARLY DONE. Done: (1) 'last good backup was N ago' surfaced. (2) backup-failed/backup-stale rules + metrics + Collector.enrichBackup. (3) telemetry+alert evaluation LOOP wired (background.go). (4) SCHEDULED BACKUPS RUN: full+incremental cron jobs; Manager.backupMu single-flight = CodeConflict skip on overlap (no fail row/false alert). (5) off-host S3 nudge test-locked. (6) restore-test SURFACING (restoreTestStatus + RestoreTestStatus banner). (7) ***restore-test EXECUTION DONE: Manager.RestoreTest runs `pgbackrest verify` (VerifyCmd, command.go verifyTimeout=2h); handler wired; recordRestoreTest helper mirrors recordBackup; verify is READ-ONLY so it does NOT take backupMu (correct — won't corrupt). verify ≠ full restore, so verified_rows stays 0 (honest).*** (8) ***restore-test JOB WIRED (iter 18): startBackgroundJobs now registers a `restore-test` cron job on cfg.Schedules.RestoreTest via a generic registerJob helper; scheduledRestoreTest calls Manager.RestoreTest directly and deliberately SKIPS ensureBackupConfigured (its stanza-create exclusive lock + possible PG restart would collide with a long full backup still running at 05:00) — verify is a pure reader.*** (9) ***restore-test DEEP proof CORE DONE (iter 19): Manager.RestoreTestDeep — non-destructive scratch restore (os.MkdirTemp, --pg1-path override ≠ live dir) + pg_ctl boot (full WAL replay, private socket, archive_mode=off) + real row count into verified_rows + guaranteed teardown (deferred stop+RemoveAll). Headroom-gated (free>=dbSize×1.25, CodeSafety refusal issues no restore). Opt-in POST /backups/restore-test?deep=true. Unit-tested via fake Runner with injectable seams (scratchRoot/diskFree/resolvePGBin). Reviewer-fixed 2 vacuous assertions + space-safe socket quoting.*** REMAINING 1.5: (a) DEEP end-to-end INTEGRATION test (needs a populated pgBackRest repo + real PG to prove restore+boot+count against real binaries — the one thing fake-Runner can't cover; integration-tagged, env-gated like readonly_integration_test.go; WON'T run in the loop's `go test ./...` gate by design). (c) the `digest` job is still unregistered, but NO digest builder exists yet — not load-bearing for 'never lose data', defer to band 2+. DONE iter 20: (b) DEEP UI opt-in button on Backups page (DeepRestoreTestConfirm; calls ?deep=true; verified_rows>0 'rows restored and verified' branch already renders). After (a), band 1.5 is complete → move to band 2 (stability). NOTE (a) is env-gated so it never proves green in the normal loop gate — pick it only when you can stand up a throwaway PG+pgBackRest stanza, else move to band 2. Optionally wire OTLP exporter (nil in collector). Plumbing: alert engine reads Snapshot FIELDS via alert/metrics.go metricValue. Backups: internal/backup/manager.go (Backup takes backupMu.TryLock→ownership→terminal fail/success; RestoreTest verifyForRead→VerifyCmd→pass/fail row); internal/store/backup.go (ListBackups/ListRestoreTests newest-first, LatestSuccessfulBackup, InsertRestoreTest). Frontend SUCCESS_RESULTS={success,ok,completed,pass}/FAILURE_RESULTS={fail,failed,error} in Backups.tsx — Manager writes 'success'/'fail'. Scheduler: internal/scheduler — empty spec disables. go/psql/pg_ctl snap-sandboxed (run sandbox-disabled). TMPDIR empty in some shells — use /tmp.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
